name: Heading Hierarchy Logical
description: Verifies headings follow the H1 → H2 → H3 sequence without skipped levels. Proper hierarchy helps screen readers, search engine parsing, and AI extractors that use headings as section boundaries.

criteria.pass: Headings follow H1 → H2 → H3 sequence with no skipped levels (no H1 → H3)
criteria.fail: Multiple skipped levels or no logical structure

fix_template: Restructure headings to follow sequential order. Each H3 must be under an H2. Each H4 under an H3.
sources: Proper heading hierarchy helps screen readers and search engine content parsing. AI extractors use headings to identify section boundaries and topics.

name: Primary Keyword in First 100 Words
description: Verifies the primary keyword (from title or target query) appears in the opening 100 words of body text. Google's passage-level indexing and AI answer selection weight opening content heavily.
method_detail: Extract first 100 body words, check keyword

criteria.pass: Primary keyword (from title or target query) appears in the first 100 words of body text
criteria.warn: Close semantic variant appears but not exact keyword
criteria.fail: Neither keyword nor variant in first 100 words

fix_template: Incorporate primary keyword naturally in the opening paragraph
sources: Google uses passage-level indexing — first 100 words strongly signal page topic. AI extractors weight opening content heavily for answer selection.

name: Three or More Internal Links
description: Verifies the body content contains at least 3 internal links. Internal links distribute PageRank, help crawlers discover pages, and signal topical relationships; pages without them are potential orphans.

criteria.pass: 3+ `<a href>` links pointing to same-domain pages in body content (not nav/footer)
criteria.fail: Zero internal links in body content

fix_template: Add contextual internal links to related pages within body content
sources: Internal links distribute PageRank, help crawlers discover pages, and signal topical relationships. Pages with no internal links are potential orphans.

name: Descriptive Anchor Text
description: Verifies anchor text is descriptive rather than generic ("click here", "read more"). Google uses anchor text to understand linked page context; generic anchors provide zero context.

criteria.pass: Zero anchors with text "click here", "read more", "here", "link", "this" as standalone text

fix_template: Replace "Click here to learn more" with "[Topic name] guide" or "[Descriptive phrase]"
sources: Google uses anchor text to understand linked page context. "Click here" provides zero context. Descriptive anchors help both users and search engines.

name: All Images Have Alt Text
description: Verifies every img tag has alt text (decorative images may use alt=""). Required for accessibility (WCAG 2.1), used by Google Image Search, and may aid AI content understanding.
method_detail: Count img without alt or alt=""

criteria.pass: All `` tags have non-empty `alt` attribute (decorative images may use `alt=""`)
criteria.fail: 4+ images missing alt text or > 50% of images lack alt

fix_template: Add descriptive `alt="[What the image shows]"` to each img tag
sources: Alt text is required for accessibility (WCAG 2.1). Used by Google Image Search for indexing. AI crawlers may use alt text for content understanding.

name: Sufficient Word Count
description: Verifies word count meets the page-type minimum and is calibrated against the competitor median. Insufficient content means fewer passages available for passage-level ranking.
method_detail: Word count vs page-type threshold AND competitor median

criteria.pass: Word count meets page-type threshold AND is within 50% of competitor median. Page-type thresholds: Homepage 200+, Landing 500+, Blog/Article 800+, Product 300+, Service 500+, HowTo 600+
criteria.warn: Meets page-type threshold but significantly below competitor median (> 50% less)
criteria.fail: Below page-type minimum threshold (Homepage 200+, Landing 500+, Blog/Article 800+, Product 300+, Service 500+, HowTo 600+)

fix_template: Expand content to cover topics competitors address that you don't. Don't pad — add substance.
sources: Passage-level ranking means word count alone doesn't win. But insufficient content means fewer passages to rank for. Competitor median provides real-world calibration. A 1,500-word page is fine absolutely but weak if competitors average 4,500.

name: No Keyword Stuffing
description: Verifies primary keyword density stays below 3%. Density above 4% is a negative signal; the GEO paper found keyword stuffing had a negative impact on AI visibility.

criteria.na: Applies to blog, article, howto only. Not scored for homepage/landing/product.

fix_template: Replace some keyword instances with synonyms or related terms
sources: Outdated as a primary concern — Google's semantic understanding handles synonyms. But density > 4% is still a negative signal. GEO paper: keyword stuffing had NEGATIVE impact on visibility.

name: Outbound Links to Authoritative Sources
description: Verifies the body content links out to authoritative external sources. The GEO paper found "Cite Sources" is the highest-impact visibility strategy (30-40% boost), and AI engines cross-check claims against multiple sources.

criteria.pass: 2+ outbound links to authoritative external sources (.gov, .edu, academic, official documentation, recognized industry sources) in body content
criteria.fail: Zero outbound links in body content

fix_template: Add 2-3 outbound links to studies, official documentation, or authoritative sources that support claims made in the content
sources: GEO paper (KDD 2024): "Cite Sources" strategy = 30-40% visibility boost — the highest-impact GEO strategy. Outbound citations signal trust and enable AI cross-reference verification. All AI engines cross-check claims against multiple sources.

name: Clean URL Structure
description: Verifies the URL path is short, lowercase, hyphenated, and free of session IDs or non-readable identifiers. A minor signal — clean URLs aid user comprehension and sharing.

criteria.pass: URL path < 75 characters, uses hyphens, lowercase, no special characters or session IDs
criteria.fail: Path > 100 characters, contains query parameters as primary URL, or non-readable IDs

sources: Minor signal. Clean URLs are better for user comprehension and sharing. Google handles ugly URLs fine technically.

name: Open Graph Tags Complete
description: Verifies og:title, og:description, og:image, and og:type are all present. Not a ranking signal but critical for social sharing appearance and CTR; AI systems may reference OG data for entity understanding.
fix_template: Add: `<meta property="og:title" content="[Title]">` + description + image + type
sources: Not a ranking signal. Critical for social sharing appearance. Affects CTR from social platforms. AI systems may reference OG data for entity understanding.

name: Twitter Card Tags
description: Verifies twitter:card markup is present, preferably summary_large_image. A minor signal affecting appearance when shared on X/Twitter; not a ranking signal.

criteria.warn: Twitter card present but using `summary` instead of `summary_large_image`

sources: Minor. Affects appearance when shared on X/Twitter. Not a ranking signal.

name: Visible Publication/Update Date
description: Verifies a publication or update date is both visible in content and present in schema. Freshness is a primary AI citation signal — Perplexity gives 3.2x more citations to content under 30 days old.
method_detail: Scan content for date patterns + check schema dates

criteria.pass: Date visible on page (in content, not just schema) AND datePublished or dateModified in schema
criteria.warn: Date in schema but not visible on page, or visible but not in schema
criteria.fail: No date anywhere (neither visible nor schema)

fix_template: Add visible "Published: [date]" or "Last updated: [date]" near top of content. Add datePublished/dateModified to schema.
sources: Google API leak: three date signals (`bylineDate`, `syntacticDate`, `semanticDate`). Perplexity: content within 30 days gets 3.2x more citations. 83% of commercial AI citations from pages < 12 months old.

name: No Broken External Links
description: Verifies outbound links in the body content resolve with 200 status. Broken citations are worthless for AI cross-reference verification and erode trust signals.
method_detail: WebFetch each outbound link, check for 4xx/5xx

criteria.pass: All outbound links in body content return 200 status
criteria.na: No outbound links (page fails C8 instead)

fix_template: Replace broken links with current URLs to the same or equivalent authoritative sources. If the source no longer exists, find an alternative or remove the citation.
sources: Broken outbound links erode trust signals. If the page cites a source that no longer exists, the citation is worthless for AI cross-reference verification. All three major tools (Screaming Frog, Ahrefs, Lighthouse) check broken links. GEO paper: "Cite Sources" is the #1 visibility strategy — but only if the citations actually work.
